_comment: Pluto airband channel plan + front-end config (the OPERATIONAL source of truth). Copy this file to the ROOT of the Pluto's SD card as 'airband.json' (the card must be FAT32 -- the kernel has no exFAT). On boot the init script mounts the card at /mnt/sdcard and maia-httpd loads it via --airband-config /mnt/sdcard/airband.json; the web config UI (/airband.html) reads and writes THIS file. If the card is missing/unformatted/unmounted, maia-httpd falls back to its built-in default (a single channel, 118.050 AWOS, at 0 dB) -- hearing only a faint, single AWOS channel is the obvious cue that this SD plan did not load. NOTE: 118.050 (S50 AWOS) is intentionally NOT in this operational plan -- it is a continuous AWOS carrier we don't want to track/stream; it remains only as the built-in fallback indicator above. samp_rate MUST be the value the channelizer was built for (audio rate = samp_rate / 160 / 5 = 20000 sps at the 16 MHz build; center_hz 126.4 MHz puts all 18 channels, including 133.65, inside +/- 8 MHz). channels_hz: up to 18 (N_CHANNELS) absolute channel centers, all within center_hz +/- samp_rate/2; the FPGA always frames 18 positional channels, so any positions beyond this list are stale and the host reader must run --channels = len(channels_hz). channel_labels: optional, parallel to channels_hz, cosmetic (web UI only). gain_db: fixed manual RX gain -- ADJUSTABLE. Edit this value to suit your front end, then restart the receiver (the firmware's built-in fallback, used only when no SD card is present, is 0 dB). The receiver is internal-noise-limited (idle floor the same with antenna or 50 ohm load), so gain_db only needs to lift voice clear of that floor: with an external LNA ~12 dB is plenty; a bare front end needs more, up to the ~48 dB ADC-clipping knee (this file presently ships 30 dB for a bare front end). See firmware/diagnostics/ and SPUR-INVESTIGATION.md.
